problem_statement: GET /flights/search with a destination airport code returns no matches because the destination filter is likely doing a case-sensitive comparison against stored airport codes, while request input may be lowercase (e.g. jfk) and airport codes are stored uppercase.
goal: Make flight search by destination airport return the correct flights regardless of the casing of the supplied destination airport code.
acceptance_criteria: Searching /flights/search?destination=jfk returns flights whose destination airport code is JFK.
Destination filtering behaves case-insensitively for airport codes.
Existing search behavior for other filters remains unchanged.
constraints: Keep the fix localized to the flight search service if possible.
Airport codes are stored in upper case, so normalization should happen before comparison or at query time.
unknowns: Whether the comparison is performed in application code or in a database query inside search_flights.
Whether origin and other airport-code filters have the same casing issue and should be aligned.
repo_summary: Backend service centered around flight search and related API/routes, with tests under backend/app/tests. Likely implementation in backend/app/services/flight_service.py, with route wiring in backend/app/api/routes/flights.py and tests in backend/app/tests/test_flight_service.py.

targeted_symbols.reason: Issue explicitly points to this method; likely destination filter comparison needs case normalization here.
Add or adjust a focused test that proves destination search works for lowercase input against uppercase stored codes.

localization_rationale: The bug description matches a classic case-sensitivity mismatch between request input and stored airport codes.
The issue explicitly names backend/app/services/flight_service.py (search_flights) as the affected file, making this the smallest relevant area.
A unit/service test is the most direct way to verify the fix without broad changes to routing or data models.
